statement: Nvidia is expanding a vendor-financing/backstop program — backstopping GPU purchases in exchange for revenue sharing and agreeing to rent back unused GPUs at fixed rates — with early adopters Firmus (170,000 GPUs) and Sharon AI (40,000 GB300), about 210,000 GPUs combined, following CoreWeave ($6.3B) and Lambda ($1.5B) support deals.
why: By backstopping GPU purchases and guaranteeing rent-back, Nvidia underwrites committed demand for its GPUs, supporting near-term volumes.

impact.mechanism: Vendor financing converts customer GPU purchases into committed backlog for Nvidia, boosting demand but tying Nvidia revenue to the health of financed neoclouds.

evidence.excerpt: Firmus with 170,000 GPUs deploying in Batam, Indonesia, and Sharon AI with 40,000 GB300 GPUs — 210,000 GPUs combined

confidence.basis: Single secondary outlet; GPU counts given but no dollar figures for the new Firmus/Sharon commitments.

dispersion: conflicting same-key reports: statement/trend changed (trend rising -> rising); sources: Data Center Dynamics, SharonAI Holdings (SEC Form 8-K exhibit 99.1)
